label: Fireflies
description: Arcade.dev tools for interacting with Fireflies

tools.name: AddNotetakerToLiveMeeting
tools.description:
Send the Fireflies notetaker into a live meeting to capture it.

The bot can take up to a minute to join after a successful dispatch.

parameters.name: meeting_link
parameters.description: The live meeting URL for the notetaker to join.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Title for the meeting. Leave empty to let Fireflies infer one.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Minutes the notetaker should stay (15-120). Defaults to 60.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Spoken-language code (e.g. 'en'). Leave empty for auto-detection.

tools.name: AnalyzeTeamMeetings
tools.description:
Aggregate team conversation metrics and a per-speaker breakdown over a date range.

Use this to spot team-level trends across meetings in a window no wider than 30 days.
Aggregates are rolled up from each meeting's own analytics, so the sentiment and
talk-time reflect every call in the window (not just the calls of mapped team users).
The most recent 50 meetings in the range are aggregated; when more fall in the range the
result's ``truncated`` flag is set so partial aggregates are not read as complete.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Start of the range to aggregate over (ISO 8601 UTC, YYYY-MM-DD or full date-time). The range from_date..to_date may span at most 30 days.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: End of the range to aggregate over (ISO 8601 UTC, YYYY-MM-DD or full date-time). Must be on or after from_date, and the window may span at most 30 days. A date-only value includes the whole named day (meetings through end of that day), which counts toward the 30-day span.

tools.name: AskAboutMeeting
tools.description:
Ask a grounded question about a meeting and get an AI answer with suggested follow-ups.

Prefer this over reading the full transcript when the user has a specific question.
Pass the returned thread id back as thread_id to continue the same conversation.

parameters.name: question
parameters.description: The plain-language question to answer from the meeting.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting to ask about. Required when starting a new conversation; leave empty when continuing an existing thread.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Continue a prior conversation by passing the thread id from an earlier answer. Leave empty to start a new conversation about the meeting.

question.value: What action items were assigned to the engineering team during the meeting?

tools.name: CreateSoundbite
tools.description:
Clip a soundbite from a time range within a recorded meeting.

The new clip processes asynchronously; the result reports its initial processing
state. Use the soundbite read tools to retrieve it once ready.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id to clip from.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Clip start, in seconds from the start of the recording.

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Clip end, in seconds from the start of the recording.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the soundbite. Leave empty to let Fireflies pick one.

parameters.name: media_type
parameters.description: Format of the clip. Defaults to video. A video clip requested from an audio-only recording is automatically created as audio instead (the result detail notes the switch), so the clip never silently fails to process.

tools.name: GetMeetingAnalytics
tools.description:
Get per-speaker talk-time and pace plus the sentiment breakdown for one meeting.

Use this to gauge how a call went -- who dominated, speaking pace, and overall
sentiment. Analytics may be sparse or empty for very short calls.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id.

tools.name: GetMeetingSummary
tools.description:
Get Fireflies' AI-generated notes for a meeting: overview, action items, keywords, outline.

Use this single call to answer "what were the takeaways / action items from this
call?"; it is far cheaper and more reliable than reading the verbatim transcript.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id.

tools.name: GetMeetingTranscript
tools.description:
Get the verbatim spoken lines of a meeting, in chronological order with speaker attribution.

Fireflies serves a meeting's lines only as a whole, so each call retrieves the full
transcript; limit, offset, and the from_time/to_time window then bound what is returned
to keep the response small (they do not reduce what is fetched upstream). Transcripts can
run to thousands of lines, so request a narrow window. To quote a moment found via the AI
question or notes tools (which report timestamps in seconds), pass from_time and/or to_time;
offset and limit then apply within that window. For takeaways or action items, prefer the AI
meeting-notes tool over reading raw lines.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum lines to return, starting from the beginning of the recording (1-500). Defaults to 200.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of lines to skip from the start (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: from_time
parameters.description: Only return lines spoken at or after this many seconds into the recording. Leave unset for no lower bound.

parameters.name: to_time
parameters.description: Only return lines spoken at or before this many seconds into the recording. Leave unset for no upper bound.

tools.name: GetSoundbite
tools.description:
Get a single soundbite by id.

Returns a graceful not-found result instead of raising for an unknown or
inaccessible id, so lookups compose like the list/search tools.

parameters.name: bite_id
parameters.description: The soundbite's id.

tools.name: ListActionItems
tools.description:
Gather follow-ups from recent meetings into one flat list to answer "what do I owe?".

Each meeting's AI action-items notes are flattened into individual, owner-attributed
items so the caller's outstanding follow-ups across many calls surface in one request
rather than by opening each meeting. Prefer this over reading each call's notes
separately when triaging open commitments; set assigned_to_me to narrow to the caller's
own follow-ups, or assignee_email to narrow to a specific teammate's.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Only include meetings on or after this UTC date-time (ISO 8601). Leave empty for no lower bound.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: Only include meetings on or before this UTC date-time (ISO 8601). A date-only value (YYYY-MM-DD) includes the whole named day. Leave empty for no upper bound.

parameters.name: assigned_to_me
parameters.description: When true, return only follow-ups attributed to the caller. Defaults to false (every meeting's follow-ups, each flagged with whether it is the caller's).

parameters.name: assignee_email
parameters.description: Only include follow-ups attributed to this person, matched by their email handle and name (so 'shawnee@acme.com' matches items owned by 'Shawnee'). Use this to triage what a specific teammate owes. Leave empty for no assignee filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum recent meetings to scan for follow-ups (1-50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of recent meetings to skip before scanning (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListChannels
tools.description:
List the channels meetings are filed into.

A channel's id can be passed to a meeting search to narrow results to that channel, so
this is the lookup behind that filter as well as a view of how the account organizes
its calls.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum channels to return (1-50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of channels to skip from the start (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListContacts
tools.description: List the people the caller has met with, most recent first.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum contacts to return (1-50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of contacts to skip from the start (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListSoundbites
tools.description: List soundbites, optionally scoped to your own, your team's, or a single meeting's.

parameters.name: mine
parameters.description: When true, return only soundbites the caller created. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: my_team
parameters.description: When true, include soundbites shared across the caller's team. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: Keep only soundbites clipped from this meeting, applied to each fetched page. Leave empty to not filter by meeting.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum soundbites to return (1-50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position; number of soundbites to skip. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: RevokeMeetingAccess
tools.description:
Remove a previously granted teammate's access to a meeting.

You can only manage sharing on a meeting you own or manage. Revoking an email that
does not currently have access is not a silent no-op: Fireflies may return a
``failed`` status, so treat a failed result as "no change was made" rather than a hard
error.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id to revoke access to.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address whose access should be removed.

tools.name: SearchMeetings
tools.description:
Find a recorded meeting by its title, the people on it, when it happened, or its topic.

Use this single call to locate a past call; results are newest first. By default the
keyword matches the meeting title only; switch match to content to find a call by its
spoken topics -- that scans each recent meeting's AI notes (gist, summary, keywords,
topics, action items) and keeps the ones whose notes or title contain the keyword.
Each result carries a one-line AI gist for triage; set include_notes to also pull each
call's short summary and action items in the same request (the standup-prep digest).

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Text to match. Matched against the meeting title, or against what was discussed (the AI notes) when content matching is selected. Leave empty for no keyword filter.

parameters.name: from_date
parameters.description: Only include meetings on or after this UTC date-time (ISO 8601). Leave empty for no lower bound.

parameters.name: to_date
parameters.description: Only include meetings on or before this UTC date-time (ISO 8601). A date-only value (YYYY-MM-DD) includes the whole named day. Leave empty for no upper bound.

parameters.name: host_email
parameters.description: Only include meetings hosted by this email. Leave empty to not filter by host.

parameters.name: organizer_email
parameters.description: Only include meetings organized by this email. Leave empty to not filter by organizer.

parameters.name: participant_email
parameters.description: Only include meetings this email participated in. Leave empty to not filter by participant.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Only include meetings filed under this channel id. Leave empty to not filter by channel.

parameters.name: mine
parameters.description: When true, restrict to the caller's own meetings. Defaults to false (all accessible).

parameters.name: match
parameters.description: How the keyword is matched. Defaults to matching the meeting title (fast, exact, across all history). Switch to content matching to find a call by what was discussed when the title is generic or unknown; content matching scans the AI notes of the most recent meetings in the requested window.

parameters.name: include_notes
parameters.description: When true, also return each meeting's short summary and action items so a 'catch me up since <date>' digest can be built in one call. Defaults to false (only the one-line gist).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum meetings to return (1-50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Number of matching meetings to skip (0-indexed). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ShareMeeting
tools.description:
Grant one or more teammates access to a meeting's notes and recording.

You can only share a meeting you own or manage. Re-sharing an email that already has
access is not a silent no-op: Fireflies may return a ``failed`` status, so treat a
failed result as "no change was made" rather than relying on this call to idempotently
ensure access.

parameters.name: transcript_id
parameters.description: The meeting's transcript id to share.

parameters.name: emails
parameters.description: Email addresses to grant access to.

parameters.name: expiry_days
parameters.description: Days until the granted access expires. Use 0 for access that never expires; a negative value is rejected. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: UploadRecording
tools.description:
Queue an already-recorded audio or video file for Fireflies transcription.

Use this for a recording that exists elsewhere; use the live-notetaker tool to
capture a meeting happening now. Transcription runs asynchronously after queuing.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Publicly accessible https URL of the audio or video recording to transcribe.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Title for the recording. Leave empty to let Fireflies infer one.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Spoken-language code (e.g. 'en'). Leave empty for auto-detection.

parameters.name: save_video
parameters.description: When true, retain the source video alongside the transcript. Defaults to false.

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description: Get the caller's own Fireflies identity and usage summary.

summary:
Fireflies is an AI meeting assistant platform; this toolkit lets Arcade agents interact with Fireflies to capture, search, analyze, and share meeting recordings and transcripts.

## Capabilities

- **Meeting capture & ingestion**: Dispatch the Fireflies notetaker bot into a live meeting or upload an existing audio/video file for async transcription.
- **Search & retrieval**: Find past meetings by title, participants, date, or spoken content; list channels, contacts, and soundbites with flexible scoping filters.
- **Summaries, transcripts & Q&A**: Fetch AI-generated notes (overview, action items, keywords), retrieve verbatim transcripts with time-windowing, or ask grounded natural-language questions with conversational follow-up.
- **Analytics**: Get per-speaker talk-time, speaking pace, and sentiment for individual meetings, or aggregate those metrics across a team's calls over a rolling 30-day window.
- **Collaboration**: Share or revoke meeting access with teammates; clip soundbites from recordings for sharing or reference.
- **Account introspection**: Retrieve the caller's own Fireflies identity and usage summary.

## Secrets

`FIREFLIES_API_KEY` — A personal API key issued by Fireflies that authenticates every GraphQL request made on behalf of your account. To obtain one: log in to [Fireflies](https://app.fireflies.ai), open **Integrations → API Access** (or navigate directly to `https://app.fireflies.ai/api-integrations`), and generate or copy your API key. The key is tied to your user account and carries full access to your meetings and team data, so treat it as a credential. See the [Fireflies API docs](https://docs.fireflies.ai/getting-started/authentication) for details.

Store the key as an Arcade secret. See the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) and the [Arcade secrets dashboard](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
